target.name: Nominal
target.founder: Cameron McCord, Bryce Strauss, Jason Hoch

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in AI & Software. Range: $2.0B - $3.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
Nominal is a Series B-2 stage AI & Software company currently valued at $1.0B following $155M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws on a peer set spanning 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir), with direct analysis anchored to 3 same-sector, similar-valuation-tier private peers: Ayar Labs (Series E, $3.8B), Black Forest Labs (Series B, $3.2B), and Field AI (IPO-stage, $2.0B).

The peer-median method is applicable and yields an implied valuation of $3.2B, representing the median across the three-company comp set. This method establishes a floor at $2.0B (Field AI) and ceiling at $3.8B (Ayar Labs). Given the limited methodological diversity—only one triangulation method is sufficiently supported by available data—the analysis rests materially on peer multiples rather than alternative valuation frameworks.

The triangulated range is $2.8B to $3.7B, with a median of $3.2B. This implies an upside of 220% from the current $1.0B valuation. Key uncertainties include the heterogeneity of peer stages (Nominal sits between Series B and IPO comparables), the thinness of the comp set, and the absence of revenue or growth-rate normalization across peers. The valuation is most defensible at the median but warrants material discount if Nominal's unit economics or growth trajectory diverge materially from the peer cohort.
